evidence_state: No external pilot session has been completed or recorded.
purpose: Determine whether a non-owner formal-research lead has the repeated research-state problem KeyAI targets, can orient in the reference environment, and can define a bounded adapter test worth building under TASK-012.

roles: Technical lead of an AI-for-mathematics or formalization team
Maintainer of a multi-week Lean project with human and AI contributors
required_signals: The team has a real, repeated workflow involving claims, evidence, tasks, and verifier results.
At least two people or agents contribute across more than one session.
The participant can provide a public or sanitized summary for qualification.
The participant can attend one observed onboarding session and make an explicit build, change, stop, or pending decision.
out_of_scope: Requests to solve a cryptographic key or recover a secret.
A one-off proof request with no durable research-state problem.
A public intake that requires secrets, credentials, private keys, internal architecture, or confidential workflow details.
A request for a finished hosted, authenticated, multi-project product.

privacy_and_safety.public_intake: The GitHub intake is public and collects qualification signals only. Detailed workflow mapping is never required in a public issue.
privacy_and_safety.authorization_attestation: Any cryptographic instance must be synthetic, a published challenge, or owned and explicitly authorized by the participant. The pilot does not accept requests involving live funds, accounts, third-party assets, or key recovery.
privacy_and_safety.collection_policy: Record only the minimum sanitized facts needed for the product decision. Do not commit raw private interview notes or participant contact details.
privacy_and_safety.retention_policy: The repository retains only participant-approved sanitized summaries and decision evidence. Observational evidence is labeled observational and is not presented as machine verification.
privacy_and_safety.incident_response: If sensitive material is posted, stop the intake, hide or remove the exposed content through repository moderation, tell the participant to rotate any affected secret, and record only a sanitized incident disposition.
privacy_and_safety.task_authority: TASK-011 authorizes qualification, observation, and workflow mapping only. It does not authorize executing a new research or cryptanalytic candidate.
privacy_and_safety.experiment_gate: A second-project execution requires TASK-012 authorization. Any secp256k1 or ECDLP experiment additionally requires a superseding route-selection decision, a selected route, and a promoted task and hypothesis with success and stop conditions.
prohibited_inputs: confidential repositories or unpublished material without permission
internal workflow or architecture details not approved for public disclosure
live-funds, account-recovery, or third-party cryptographic targets
privacy_and_safety.reference_boundary: The secp256k1 reference environment maps and verifies a research boundary. It does not solve the plain secp256k1 discrete logarithm problem.

session_plan.label: Qualify
session_plan.objective: Confirm the primary user, repeated workflow, authorization basis, sanitized boundary, and decision owner.
evidence_to_capture: authorization basis for any cryptographic material
session_plan.exit_gate: Proceed only when the pain is repeated, the qualification summary is safe to retain, and a discovery disposition can be made.

session_plan.label: Orient
session_plan.objective: Observe the participant using the current secp256k1 workspace without a guided tour.
evidence_to_capture: time to state the current decision
time to identify the main blocker
time to identify the active next action
questions, wrong turns, and unclear labels
session_plan.exit_gate: Record whether the participant can explain current state, blocker, and next action in 10 minutes or less. Treat this as a usability diagnostic, not product-value validation.

session_plan.label: Map workflow
session_plan.objective: Map one sanitized real workflow into ingest, structure, decide, execute, verify, and retain without executing a candidate.
evidence_to_capture: where context or provenance is lost
session_plan.exit_gate: The participant identifies a repeated failure and the smallest adapter boundary that could test it under TASK-012.

session_plan.label: Set disposition
session_plan.objective: Choose build, change, stop, or pending and record the narrowest justified next step.
evidence_to_capture: explicit build, change, stop, or pending decision
minimum adapter boundary when build is selected
missing evidence when pending is selected
session_plan.exit_gate: A discovery decision is recorded without treating interest, scheduling, or orientation success as retention or MVP validation.

measurements.operational_definition: Elapsed observed time until the participant correctly states current decision, main blocker, and active next action.
The participant names a failure that recurs across sessions or contributors and describes its present workaround.
measurements.target: One specific repeated workflow with a concrete consequence

measurements.operational_definition: A sanitized real workflow maps to the six-stage product loop and exposes a smallest adapter boundary without executing a candidate.
measurements.target: One bounded adapter contract or an explicit reason the loop does not fit
measurements.evidence_source: Sanitized workflow map and discovery disposition

measurements.operational_definition: The project lead records exactly one build, change, stop, or pending outcome under the priority rules.
measurements.target: One dated disposition linked to CH-001

measurements.operational_definition: The same team returns for an observed second-project session; a promise or scheduled date alone is not completion.
measurements.target: One completed return session under TASK-012; not required to close TASK-011 discovery

measurements.operational_definition: Every promoted pilot artifact links to source, task, decision, trust classification, and the applicable verifier or validation method. Observational evidence is labeled observational.
Number of KeyAI generator-code edits required to represent the second project.
measurements.target: Zero for the MVP; discover the expected boundary in TASK-011 and measure it only in TASK-012

measurements.operational_definition: Secrets, credentials, private keys, personal data, or confidential material placed in the public intake or evidence log.

build: The participant demonstrates the repeated research-state pain.
The sanitized workflow maps to the product loop and exposes a minimum adapter boundary.
The participant authorizes a bounded TASK-012 test; no execution is performed in TASK-011.
change: The pain is real but the current workspace or language blocks orientation.
The workflow maps to the product loop but requires a narrower user, verifier, or artifact contract.
A specific product or trust-boundary change is required before an adapter test can be justified.
stop: The problem is a one-off proof request rather than durable research-state coordination.
The request involves unauthorized cryptographic targets, live funds, secrets, or unsafe data handling.
No repeated pain or workflow fit is identified.
pending: The participant appears relevant but the available evidence is insufficient or cannot yet be sanitized.
A decision owner, authorization basis, or bounded workflow map is still missing.
Pending does not unlock TASK-012 and is not evidence of retention.
decision_rules.priority: Apply stop for safety or authorization failure; otherwise pending for insufficient evidence; otherwise change when a concrete revision is required; select build only when every build condition is recorded.
decision_rules.minimum_evidence: One observed discovery session, one explicit build/change/stop/pending disposition for CH-001, and no customer or retention claim beyond the recorded behavior.

validated_when: A non-owner completes ingest, structure, decide, execute, verify, and retain on a second project.
The same team returns for a completed follow-up session.
Every promoted artifact satisfies provenance, trust-classification, and applicable-verifier requirements.

record_constraints: Every sequence is a unique positive integer; records are stored in sequence order and performed_on dates never move backward.
Every case_id is an opaque repository-safe identifier that links discovery, second-project, and return evidence without storing participant identity.
Discovery records belong to TASK-011; second_project and return records belong to TASK-012.
Every record has a non-empty participant-approved public summary.
Every completed record links at least one existing sanitized regular file inside this repository.
evidence_log_schema.evidence_state_policy: When no completed external evidence record exists, use the exact no-session state. Otherwise name the latest record id, sequence, date, case_id, task_id, stage, hypothesis, and disposition when applicable.

primary_hypothesis_lifecycle.unvalidated: No completed external CH-001 discovery record exists.
primary_hypothesis_lifecycle.testing: The latest completed external CH-001 discovery disposition is build or change.
primary_hypothesis_lifecycle.rejected: The latest completed external CH-001 discovery disposition is stop.
primary_hypothesis_lifecycle.supported: The latest CH-001 discovery disposition is build and the same opaque case completes an ordered TASK-012 second_project and return sequence.

evidence_log_schema.completed_discovery_definition: Count only an external CH-001 record with stage discovery, status completed, disposition build, change, or stop, a non-empty public summary, and at least one existing sanitized evidence file.
evidence_log_schema.task_012_unlock_definition: Only the latest completed external CH-001 discovery disposition may control TASK-012, and it unlocks only when that latest disposition is build. A later change, stop, or pending disposition relocks TASK-012.
evidence_log_schema.completed_external_pilot_definition: Count only an ordered external CH-001 TASK-012 second_project record for the same opaque case as the latest build discovery, with status completed, a non-empty public summary, and at least one existing sanitized repository evidence file. Qualification, discovery, scheduling, and cancellation never count as completed pilots.
